title: Workflow design to check for a duplicate Item Name in a board
excerpt: Has anyone been able to leverage workflows to check for duplicates on a board? I’m running into an issue where I can quite get it to work right.

posts.author: DanCrum (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone been able to leverage workflows to check for duplicates on a board? I’m running into an issue where I can quite get it to work right.

posts.author: sirah (Sirah E)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @DanCrum Sirah here from Mindflows!

This depends on your use case but have you tried to use the filter icon to check for duplicates?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have? ie CRM has a setting option to check for duplicates

image566×208 17.9 KB

posts.author: DanCrum (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s ENT Work Management, which is why i’m using Workflows to use it.

posts.author: DanCrum (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: Sadly, that isn’t an option. The client wants a real time update at the Item level to quickly designate if it’s a duplicate.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Try something like this:

image1470×738 46.1 KB

image1470×738 51.9 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Depending on how you want to be notified, you can change the last step to notify or email instead of/in addition to changing the status

posts.author: DanCrum (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml:
So I did something similar that worked with adding a connect column in the rabbit hole or satisfying the “workflow failed to run” message.

But that essentially is what I needed. I don’t know why I didn’t stop there

Thank you @Clover_Gal
